text.heading: Valentine's Day the Nigerian way
text.p: Valentine's day comes to us today and a tradition that began more than a thousand years ago will continue. This day is second only to christmas in the exchange of cards and has put majority of us on edge...some people are even praying for gifts of houses, can you imagine, on valentine's day, ha ha?

In preparing for this piece, I went in search of the history of this somewhat historical holiday. I tell you, it really is confusing...different stories from different quarters. However, one fact that is general to all the different versions of the story which is that St. Valentine's day is all about that ever popular four letter word - LOVE.

It is also suggested that the 'holiday' had originally been a pagan holiday held on the 15th of february before efforts were made by the romans to '
christianize' it...that effort can be said to have been successful, to some extent.
Enough of the history...back here in Nigeria, in the characteristic way of nigerians, we have embraced the holiday with widespread arms to the extent that, year after year, we all look forward to it.

Also, as we seem to always do with all copied stuffs, majority of us seem to misunderstand the real reason for the holiday. The complexities that surround the word LOVE is not in anyway helping our situation.

Majority of us seem to attribute the 'holiday' mainly to sexual activity, which is why I can bet my fingers that many more children will be made on the morrow. Sadly though, many diseases would be transmitted too.

On the other hand, there are those of us who believe it is a day set aside to show love...'original love' to others especially the less privileged. Couples would also be afforded the opportunity to strenghten the bond of their love although, in their own case, it is advisable that it be more than an annual event.

From whatever perspective we want to take it, tomorrow, fast food joints will witness remarkable sales, the sale of uhm...'raincoat' is expected to increase and the events holding tomorrow will be well attended.

It is even more advantageous that it falls on a saturday...I remember my secondary school days when my classmates will absent themselves from school on valentine's day...believe me, I was not amongst them...they face the consequences themselves though. Everybody will have the chance to do whatever they like...ehn, I fear for the just matured females.

Talking about events, there are quite a couple of them celebrating love which had been holding since yesterday. Gbenga Adeyinka, Z-Don and others will be at Princess Hall in Akute for Couples Day-Out.

Sincerely, one of the events that struck me most is the one organised by Continental Broadcast Service, it is a party to be held on board a yatch that will cruise the Lagos coast line from this evening till the wee hours of tomorrow. More interesting about the event is that its proceeds will be donated to charity.
Well, come what may, y'all should just make sure you have a swell time not minding your opinion of the day...CHEERS!
